past_recommendations.text: Adopt ~1.5-ATR initial stop guidance for reversal entries — covers 27/28 winners' pre-target adverse while losers run median 1.66-ATR MAE; sub-0.5-ATR stops would have knocked out 13/28 winners.

Investigate a regime/breadth gate for Large-cap reversals (e.g., sector-wide squeeze detection) — Large GO ran 7/22 with the Apr-30→May-6 semis melt-up contributing 7 correlated failures at ~3-ATR median MAE.
Track the 4/5-vs-5/5 inversion (16/29 vs 6/16) over the next batch before acting; if it persists at n≥20 per cell, evaluate whether the 5th criterion is selecting unshortable peak momentum.
Document that first-print fixed holds to D+3 have negative expectancy (mean −1.5%) — exits must be MFE-managed (1-ATR target hit in ≤1 day median) rather than time-based.
Defer all bounce-side conclusions until n ≥ 20 independent episodes (currently 7).

reversal | POOLED | prior_day_rvol | score-point only (per-cap 1.25–2.0) | hard floor: suppress/NOGO any signal with prior_day_rvol < 1.25 | 2/21 (10%) tradeable below 1.25 vs 26/46 (57%) above; within Large (n=32): 0/12 below vs 9/20 above; only 2 winners lost (WOLF 1.10, NOK 1.07); cluster-independent (2 of 21 sub-1.25 rows in the May melt-up)

reversal | POOLED | pct_from_9ema | per-cap score-point (0.04–0.80) | secondary hard floor at 0.10 pooled | ≤0.10: 2/16 (12%) vs >0.10: 26/51 (51%); after the RVOL veto it incrementally drops 7 more losers for 2 winners (AMD 0.054, TSLA 0.052, both Large); combined RVOL+9EMA gate: pass 24/37 (65%) vs fail 4/30 (13%) — adopt only if the RVOL veto alone proves insufficient, since within-Large medians barely separate (0.128 vs 0.107)
Implement the pooled prior_day_rvol ≥ 1.25 hard veto on reversal signals (downgrade to NOGO/suppress, not just a lost score point) — strongest single filter found this cycle, 10% vs 57% tradeable.

Re-evaluate gap_pct as a reversal criterion next cycle — zero pooled separation (W med 0.014 vs L med 0.011, flat bins, n=67); if still flat at ~100 first-flags, replace its score point with an RVOL-tier point so the 5-point score stops rewarding noise (also a candidate cause of the 4/5 > 5/5 inversion).

Continue tracking the 4/5-vs-5/5 inversion (now 16/29 vs 6/16 first-flags, second consecutive cycle, 5/5 n=16 < 20) — check next cycle whether sub-1.25-RVOL and gap-credit signals account for the 5/5 underperformance before restructuring the score.

Reprint persistence strengthened to 28/30 (93%) after a tradeable prior print vs 18/40 (45%) otherwise — escalate Analysis #1's recommendation to surface episode_signal_num + prior-print 1-ATR status in scanner output from "recommended" to "highest-priority", with the caveat that prior tradeability must already be observable (target printed) at reprint time.

Document that D0-close-direction confirmation was tested and rejected (48% vs 37% lift, costs 14/28 winners incl. all 9 same-day payers) and that long-first tactics are unsupported (~58% long-side 1-ATR hit, adverse to thesis) — stop relitigating entry-delay variants; the edge levers are the RVOL veto, the reprint trigger, and the 1.5-ATR stop (now 27/28 winners survive, 25/39 losers capped).

Large-cap reversal weakness persists ex-cluster (9/28, 32% vs Medium 58%) — after deploying the RVOL veto (which removes all 12 sub-1.25 Large losers), re-measure Large before pursuing the breadth/regime gate from Analysis #1.

Bounce side remains deferred at 7 independent episodes (4/7 tradeable); flag for review that the lone bounce GO (CAR 5/6) was the dataset's worst signal — when n≥20, test whether the bounce score is inverted on mid-crash knife-catches (prior_day_range_atr 4.1 on that signal).

reversal | POOLED | pct_from_9ema | secondary floor 0.10 (pending) | adopt 0.10 floor as secondary gate behind the RVOL veto | combined RVOL≥1.25 & 9EMA>0.10 = 24/37 (65%) pass vs 5/31 (16%) fail; ≤0.10 alone 3/18 (17%) vs >0.10 26/50 (52%) — strengthened vs Analysis #2, but ≤0.10 cell is n=18 (<20), so adopt only if RVOL veto proves insufficient once it fires on live reversals.

Verify the VETO label appears on the first post-2026-06-10 reversal batch — the cohort is empty this cycle (no reversal signals since 2026-06-05), so the deployed veto is still unverified in production. Report its tradeable_3d rate vs GO rate next cycle.

Continue surfacing episode_signal_num + prior-print 1-ATR status in scanner output (highest-priority) — reprint-after-tradeable held at 32/34 (94%) this cycle; it remains the single strongest entry filter.

Re-measure Large-cap reversal performance only after the RVOL veto has actually fired on a live reversal batch (still 10/33 = 30% here, pre-veto data) before pursuing the Analysis #1 breadth/regime gate.
Keep tracking the 4/5 > 5/5 inversion (now 17/30 vs 6/16, 3rd cycle, 5/5 n=16 < 20) — do not restructure the score yet.

Hold all bounce-side conclusions — 18 independent episodes (6/18 tradeable); the GO-vs-CAUTION inversion (0/6 vs 6/12) is suggestive of an inverted bounce score on knife-catches but is n<20. Re-test at n≥20.
Leave gap_pct as-is — flat again this cycle (W 0.012 vs L 0.011); revisit the score-point restructure only at ~100 first-flags (currently 68 reversal first-flags).

VETO cohort still empty after 2026-06-10 (all 12 recent reversals have RVOL ≥ 1.25); the deployed veto remains unverified in production — keep reporting the vetoed tradeable_3d rate each cycle until it actually fires, and only re-examine the veto if a sub-1.25 reversal eventually appears and prints tradeable.

Large-cap reversal weakness persists at 10/36 (28%) vs Medium 61%, but this is still pre-veto-effect data — defer the Analysis #1 breadth/regime gate until the RVOL veto has actually suppressed a live Large reversal batch.
4/5 > 5/5 inversion now in its 4th cycle (58% vs 32%, 5/5 n=19) — do not restructure the score yet; re-check once 5/5 crosses n=20.
Continue surfacing episode_signal_num + prior-print 1-ATR status as highest-priority scanner output (reprint-after-tradeable held 33/35 = 94%).

Hold all bounce conclusions — 19 independent episodes (6/19 tradeable); GO-vs-CAUTION inversion (0/6 vs 6/13) is still suggestive of an inverted bounce score on knife-catches but GO cell is n=6. Re-test at n≥20.
Leave gap_pct as-is (flat: W 0.016 vs L 0.013); revisit the score-point restructure only near ~100 reversal first-flags (currently 74).

Keep reporting the VETO cohort's tradeable_3d rate every cycle; it is still empty (0 rows, all post-deploy reversals RVOL≥1.318) — flag immediately if/when it fires and print tradeable, but do NOT re-derive the veto.
Continue surfacing episode_signal_num + prior-print 1-ATR status as highest-priority scanner output — reprint-after-tradeable held at 41/45 (91%) this cycle.
Re-test the 9EMA ≤0.10 secondary floor next cycle; adopt behind the RVOL veto only once the ≤0.10 first-flag cell reaches n≥20 (currently 18).
Do not restructure the 5-point reversal score — 4/5>5/5 inversion is in its 5th cycle but 5/5 is still n=19 (<20); re-check when it crosses 20.
Hold all bounce conclusions — 26 independent episodes (7/26); GO-vs-CAUTION inversion (0/7 vs 7/19) still suggestive of an inverted bounce score on knife-catches but GO cell is n=7. Re-test at n≥20.
Defer the Analysis #1 breadth/regime gate for Large-cap reversal (10/36, 28%) until the RVOL veto has actually suppressed a live Large reversal batch.
Leave gap_pct scored as-is; revisit the RVOL-tier restructure near ~100 reversal first-flags (currently 74).

VETO cohort still empty (0 rows; min post-06-10 reversal RVOL = 1.318 vs 1.25 floor). Keep reporting its tradeable_3d rate each cycle; flag the instant a sub-1.25 reversal appears and prints tradeable. Do not re-derive.

Close the 4/5>5/5 inversion as a cap-mix confound, not a scoring defect — 5/5 is 70% Large (weak cap); within-cap the gap is noise. Stop treating the pooled inversion as evidence to restructure the 5-point score; report it cap-stratified going forward.

Re-test the 9EMA ≤0.10 secondary floor next cycle — direction is strong and stable (17% vs 51%) but the ≤0.10 cell is n=18, one short of the n≥20 gate. Adopt behind the RVOL veto the moment it reaches 20.
Continue surfacing episode_signal_num + prior-print 1-ATR status as highest-priority scanner output (reprint-after-tradeable 48/51 = 94%).
Defer the Analysis #1 breadth/regime gate for Large-cap reversal weakness (11/37 = 30%) until the RVOL veto has actually suppressed a live Large reversal batch (still pre-veto data).

Hold all bounce conclusions (29 episodes, 8/29 tradeable; GO 0/8 vs CAUTION 8/21) and leave gap_pct scored as-is (W 0.016 vs L 0.013, flat) — revisit the RVOL-tier restructure near ~100 reversal first-flags (currently 75).
Adopt the 9EMA ≤0.10 reversal floor (behind the RVOL veto) the moment its first-flag cell reaches n=20 — currently 19 (16% vs 53%); one more low-9EMA reversal clears the gate.
Continue reporting the VETO cohort tradeable_3d rate each cycle — still empty (min post-deploy RVOL 1.286); flag instantly if a sub-1.25 reversal appears and prints tradeable. Do not re-derive.
Keep surfacing episode_signal_num + prior-print 1-ATR status as the top scanner output — reprint-after-tradeable held 50/54 (93%).
Report the 4/5-vs-5/5 comparison cap-stratified going forward; do not restructure the 5-point score (pooled inversion is a cap-mix artifact).
Hold all bounce conclusions — 38 episodes, 9/38 tradeable; GO 0/12 vs CAUTION 9/26 inversion still suggestive but GO cell n=12 (<20). Re-test at n≥20.
Defer the Analysis #1 breadth/regime gate for Large-cap reversal weakness (11/38, 29%) until the RVOL veto has actually suppressed a live Large reversal batch (still pre-veto-effect data).
Leave gap_pct scored as-is (W 0.020 vs L 0.024, flat/inverted); revisit the RVOL-tier restructure near ~100 reversal first-flags (currently 78).
VETO cohort empty again (0 rows; min post-06-10 reversal RVOL 1.286) — keep reporting its tradeable_3d rate each cycle; flag instantly if a sub-1.25 reversal fires and prints tradeable.
9EMA ≤0.10 floor still gated at n=19 (16% vs 53%) — no new reversal first-flags this cycle; adopt behind the RVOL veto the moment the cell reaches 20.

NEW — treat the bounce GO 0/12 inversion as a depth confound (11/12 GO first-flags are ≤ -35% off 30d high, the 1/24 cell); report bounce results depth-stratified at -0.35 going forward, and evaluate re-keying pct_off_30d_high / pct_off_52wk_high scoring direction once the shallow first-flag cell reaches n≥20 (currently 16).

Keep episode_signal_num + prior-print 1-ATR status as the top scanner output — reprint-after-tradeable now 53/57 (93%); extend the same status line to bounce chains, where all 10 winners hit at D1+ and deep episodes resolve on print 3-4.
Note the regime flip — zero reversal signals completed after 07-02, 11 bounce first-flags in four sessions; Large-reversal regime-gate deferral unchanged (11/38, still pre-veto-effect data).
Leave gap_pct scored as-is (sixth flat cycle) and revisit the RVOL-tier restructure at ~100 reversal first-flags (still 78 — no progress this cycle).

Bounce metric re-based by the min(0.5×ATR, 6%) gate cap — invalidate/re-baseline all pre-#10 bounce conclusions in docs/signal_findings.md (GO 0/12 inversion does NOT reproduce: now GO 8/13 vs CAUTION 26/36); date-stamp the re-base and restart bounce cell counts from this cycle.
Continue reporting VETO cohort tradeable_3d each cycle — still 0 rows (min post-06-10 reversal RVOL 1.286); flag instantly if a sub-1.25 reversal fires and prints tradeable.
Adopt the 9EMA ≤ 0.10 reversal floor (behind the RVOL veto) the moment the first-flag cell reaches n=20 — held at 3/19 (15.8% vs 55.0%) for a third cycle with no new low-9EMA reversals.
Keep episode_signal_num + prior-print-tradeable status as the top scanner output — reversal reprint-after-tradeable 39/43 (91%) vs 19/41 (46%) without; bounce 51/63 (81%) under the new gate.

Data QA — CAR_reversal_2026-04-08 first-flag has mfe_atr_3d=1.22 (≥1.0 gate) and tradeable_3d=True but blank days_to_1atr; audit the days_to_1atr writer for signals whose MFE hits intraday D0 after a >1.4-ATR adverse run.

Track the regime flip — zero completed reversal signals since 07-02 window close; 11 bounce first-flags this cycle including the first 5 Large-cap bounces (3/5 tradeable, n too small); revisit Large-cap GapFade thresholds once that cell reaches n≥20.
RVOL-tier restructure still deferred — reversal first-flags at 79 of the ~100 trigger (one added this cycle); gap_pct flat for a seventh cycle.
